The backup job runs after a successful assistant turn and writes the raw transcript backup plus repo-scoped index to the dedicated backup branch.

Issue and comment bodies are not included in this report; the raw backup JSON is the canonical transcript copy. Requested backup subcommands are recorded here as issue-visible intent, then run locally against a fetched `gitclaw-backups` branch.

### Requested Command
- `verify` requested from the issue thread
- run `gitclaw backup verify --root .gitclaw/backups --repo AnandChowdhary/gitclaw` after fetching `gitclaw-backups`
- issue-side execution is metadata-only because the backup branch is written after this assistant turn

### Verification
- `gitclaw backup verify --root .gitclaw/backups --repo <owner/repo>`
- `gitclaw backup coverage --root .gitclaw/backups --repo <owner/repo> --issue <number>`
- `gitclaw backup drill --root .gitclaw/backups --repo <owner/repo> --issue <number>`
- `gitclaw backup risk --root .gitclaw/backups --repo <owner/repo>`
- `gitclaw backup provenance --root .gitclaw/backups --repo <owner/repo>`
- `gitclaw backup timeline --root .gitclaw/backups --repo <owner/repo> --limit 20`
- `gitclaw backup search --root .gitclaw/backups --repo <owner/repo> <query>`
- `gitclaw backup retention-plan --root .gitclaw/backups --repo <owner/repo> --keep-latest 50`
- validates the repo-scoped index, README, canonical issue paths, JSON schema version, counts, timestamps, traversal-safe payload paths, and git provenance; search reports hashes and metadata without printing raw backup bodies
